mission.title: Install and Configure Openebs on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenEBS is a popular open-source Container Native Storage platform that provides persistent storage for stateful applications running on Kubernetes. Installing OpenEBS allows you to leverage dynamic provisioning of storage resources, ensuring high availability and resilience for your workloads.

steps.title: Add the OpenEBS Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the OpenEBS Helm repository to your Helm client to access the charts.
```bash
helm repo add openebs https://openebs.github.io/openebs
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenEBS using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install OpenEBS in a new namespace called 'openebs' using the specified version.
```bash
helm install openebs openebs/openebs --version 4.4.0 --namespace openebs --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the OpenEBS pods are running successfully in the 'openebs' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n openebs
```

steps.title: Set resource limits for OpenEBS components
steps.description:
Edit the OpenEBS values to set resource limits for the components to ensure optimal performance.
```bash
helm upgrade openebs openebs/openebs --namespace openebs --set openebs-crds.csi.volumeSnapshots.enabled=true --set localpv-provisioner.rbac.create=true
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OpenEBS will show the OpenEBS pods running in the 'openebs' namespace when you execute `kubectl get pods -n openebs`. You will also have resource limits set for the components.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add openebs https://openebs.github.io/openebs
helm repo update
helm upgrade openebs openebs/openebs --namespace openebs --set openebs-crds.csi.volumeSnapshots.enabled=true --set localpv-provisioner.rbac.create=true

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OpenEBS release from the 'openebs' namespace using Helm. This will remove all associated resources managed by Helm.
```bash
helm uninstall openebs --namespace openebs
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) specific to OpenEBS and any persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created by OpenEBS to ensure a complete cleanup.
```bash
kubectl delete crd -l openebs.io/cas-type
kubectl delete pvc --all -n openebs
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'openebs' namespace to remove any remaining resources and verify that it has been deleted.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace openebs
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of your OpenEBS installation by exporting the existing configurations and resources.
```bash
kubectl get all -n openebs -o yaml > openebs-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the OpenEBS Helm repository and then upgrade the OpenEBS installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade openebs openebs/openebs --version 4.5.0 --namespace openebs
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the OpenEBS pods to ensure that they are running the new version and are healthy.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n openebs
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice that OpenEBS pods are stuck in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs of the affected pod for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n openebs
```

troubleshooting.title: Persistent Volume Claims not binding
troubleshooting.description:
If PVCs are not binding, verify that the storage class is correctly configured and available. Check the PVC status and describe it for more details.
```bash
kubectl get pvc -n openebs
kubectl describe pvc <pvc-name> -n openebs
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC issues preventing pod creation
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter RBAC-related errors, ensure that the RBAC resources are applied correctly. Check the roles and role bindings.
```bash
kubectl get roles -n openebs
kubectl get rolebindings -n openebs
```

troubleshooting.title: High resource usage by OpenEBS components
troubleshooting.description:
If OpenEBS components are consuming too many resources, review the resource limits set in the Helm values and adjust them accordingly.
```bash
kubectl top pods -n openebs
helm get values openebs --namespace openebs
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
